recalls.Description:
Milwaukee Tool is recalling its M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaws, specifically the models with catalog number 2826-20. These chainsaws, which feature either a 12-inch or 14-inch bar, may have a defect that prevents the chain brake from activating, leading to a potential laceration hazard. The catalog number is located on the upper right side of the nameplate, while the serial number can be found on the upper left side. The serial break level is indicated by the fourth digit of the serial number.

These battery-operated chainsaws are available as standalone tools or in kits that include M18 batteries and a charger. Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled chainsaws immediately and to contact Milwaukee Tool for a free repair. Registration for the repair process can be completed online to receive a prepaid shipping label for returning the product.
recalls.Title: Milwaukee Tool Recalls M18 FUEL Chainsaws Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Milwaukee Tool at 833-953-2012, Monday to Friday from 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, or visit their eService portal at https://service.milwaukeetool.com/support/eservice. Additional information is available at https://www.milwaukeetool.com/news/press-releases under the 2025 tab or at http://www.milwaukeetool.com/support/recall.

Products.Name: Milwaukee Tool M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaw (12" or 14" bar)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 90,860 (In addition, about 7,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Milwaukee M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaw
Recalled Milwaukee M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaw (catalog/serial number label)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the chain brake not activating, including one injury involving a lacerated finger.

Manufacturers.Name: Milwaukee Electric Tool Corp., of Brookfield, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and other home improvement stores, as well as online at homedepot.com, from March 2023 through September 2024, priced around $350 for the tool alone or $790 with batteries and charger.

Hazards.Name: Potential laceration due to chain brake failure

Remedies.Name: Free repair available; stop using the chainsaw immediately

recalls.metaDescription: Milwaukee Tool recalls M18 FUEL Chainsaws due to a laceration hazard. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair.
